hq: 7190 W Sunset Blvd #14, Los Angeles, CA 90046

system_description.overview: Valence AI is an AI-driven voice intelligence platform that enables enterprises to improve customer experience by analyzing vocal tone in real time to detect emotion signals, automate call quality review, and provide actionable insights. The platform delivers emotionally intelligent voice agents that combine natural language understanding with emotion awareness for customer support and sales calls.

products.Real-Time Emotion Detection: Voice-based emotion detection via API call; real-time or recorded audio analysis via API and SDK
products.Real-Time Emotion Analytics: Voice-based emotion detection and monitoring during live calls; integrates with CCaaS platforms, BPO systems, and enterprise workflows
products.Automated QA and Compliance Support: Automation of call quality review and scoring against client-specific rubrics
products.AI-Powered Agent Tools: Emotion-aware prompts and next-best-action guidance for human and AI agents; post-call analysis and reporting
products.Emotionally Intelligent Voice Agents: Fully automated AI voice agents with emotion detection layer, adaptive tone and context awareness
products.Enterprise Support Services: Onboarding, integration support, and ongoing scaling of emotion AI

infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices; all data processed on cloud services

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs). Network diagram shows Availability Zone A (Dev, QA, Staging VPCs) and Availability Zone B (two Prod Server VPCs), each with separate databases and API gateways.
network_architecture.api_gateway: AWS API Gateway (shown in network diagram)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with virtual private network (VPN)
network_architecture.tls: TLS with certificates from an established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; internet access restricted for all systems
network_architecture.topology: Users connect via API link through internet gateway; traffic flows through API gateway into segmented VPCs across two availability zones (Dev/QA/Staging in AZ-A, Prod servers in AZ-B), each VPC has its own database.

compute: EC2 (implied - 'virtual servers', 'Prod Server' nodes in network diagram)
application_architecture.environments: 4 environments: Development, QA, Staging, Production — all in AWS VPCs

databases: Databases in each VPC (specific engine not named)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS IAM / AWS Active Directory (passwords controlled via AWS password policy)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to the production platform; MFA required for source code repository access
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: AWS password policy: forced periodic changes, password expiry, and complexity requirements; accounts locked after repeated failed logon attempts

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (AWS KMS implied)
encryption.key_management: AWS KMS (cloud provider key management service)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (source code management tool with MFA-restricted access)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules require additional approval for every merge request moving change to production environment
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used; baseline configurations retained as code with rollback capability
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO sets up automatic patch management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations scanned daily for compliance; OS patches applied as available; Windows managed via patch management system; network device patching automatic

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Monitoring tool (unnamed) — generates alerts when thresholds met; monitors system capacity and performance
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, security configurations, permissions, and roles; logs reviewed periodically
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts to IT management for high and critical risk events; incidents tracked via change-management/ticketing application

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure, data center, hosting, IDS/IPS, KMS

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection on all production servers (continuous scan, weekly definition updates); also on Mac workstations and laptops
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; self-assessment scan prior to every release or annually
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access controls deferred to AWS as subservice organization
A1.2 - Environmental protections deferred to building management / AWS
Processing Integrity criteria - determined not relevant to system
Privacy criteria - determined not relevant to system
notable_controls: Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged users (CC6.2.2)
MFA enforced on production platform administrative accounts and source code tool (CC6.1.1, CC8.1.3)
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party (CC4.1.1)
BCDR tested annually across multiple loss scenarios (CC7.4.1, CC9.1)
Vendor risk assessment quarterly for critical vendors without SOC reports (CC9.2.1)
Baseline configurations retained as code with rollback capability (CC7.1.1)

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-AZ AWS with VPC segmentation, API gateway, IDS/IPS, VPN bastion, and IaC-based config management. No CDN, no multi-region, no named regions.

application_architecture.rationale: Four environments (Dev/QA/Staging/Prod), branch protection, MFA on source code. No languages, frameworks, or container tech disclosed.

data_layer.rationale: Per-VPC databases with multi-AZ placement, data classification policy, retention policy, per-tenant segregation. No DB engine named, no caching layer, no data warehouse, backup frequency not specified.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, IDS/IPS, firewall, anti-virus, MFA, quarterly access reviews, 1-day access revocation SLA, content filtering. No WAF, no SIEM named, no cyber insurance disclosed.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC config management, branch protection, separate environments, emergency change process, monthly patch reviews. CI/CD tool unnamed, source control system unnamed, no containers.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented, annual testing across multiple loss scenarios, multi-AZ failover (multiple servers across all AZs). No RTO/RPO stated, no multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only AWS named as a vendor. Extreme single-vendor concentration with no other subservice organizations disclosed.

overall.rationale: Small team (CEO, CTO, 3 SWEs) with solid AWS-based controls, multi-AZ, annual pen test, quarterly access reviews, and BCDR testing. Key gaps: single vendor (AWS only), no named CI/CD or source control, no languages/frameworks, no RTO/RPO, minimal tech specificity throughout.

red_flags: Extreme vendor concentration: AWS is the only subservice organization — no secondary cloud, no other infra vendors named
No WAF mentioned despite API-first product exposed to external callers
CI/CD tool and source control platform not named anywhere in the report
Programming languages and frameworks entirely undisclosed
yellow_flags: Very small team (CEO + CTO + Frontend SWE + Full-stack SWE + Backend SWE + Executive Assistant) — key person risk on CTO who signs management assertion and owns security
No CDN mentioned despite voice/API workloads that could benefit from edge delivery
No SIEM named — alerting tool generically described
green_flags: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Multi-AZ deployment with failover across all AZs
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
One-business-day access revocation SLA upon termination
MFA enforced on production admin accounts and source code repository
BCDR tested annually across multiple loss scenarios
IaC-based configuration management with rollback capability
Content filtering via proxy for all systems
Anti-virus with continuous scanning on production servers
key_observations: This is an early-stage company (5-person team) with a remarkably thorough SOC 2 Type 1 — suggests the compliance program was externally driven rather than organic
Network diagram reveals a mature architecture for a small team: two AZs, dedicated VPCs per environment (Dev, QA, Staging, Prod x2), with databases co-located per VPC
The voice/emotion-AI product processes sensitive call audio data from enterprise customers yet no specific data residency or encryption-in-transit version is disclosed
Accorp Partners is an automated/AI-assisted compliance platform — the report's generic control language is consistent with a templated SOC 2 generation approach rather than a bespoke audit
Trust criteria cover Security only, despite the product's relevance to Availability (real-time call processing) and Confidentiality (sensitive conversation data)
